name: Ahmed ElSamman
tagline: Frontend Software Engineer building scalable enterprise-grade applications with Angular 21, Nx monorepos, SSR, SignalR realtime, and multi-tenant architectures.
shortBio: Frontend Software Engineer specialized in enterprise-grade Angular applications — multi-tenant systems, SSR-enabled apps, real-time features, RBAC layers, and reusable component-driven architectures in Nx monorepo environments. Currently shipping Angular 21 + SignalR + bilingual RTL features at Medica Scope.

identityNarrative.heading: From ERP systems to enterprise frontend
identityNarrative.body: I started inside business systems — building and refactoring Odoo ERP modules at Sys Gate. That work taught me to model real businesses in code: domain boundaries, audit trails, and reliability under load.

identityNarrative.heading: Into scalable Angular architecture
identityNarrative.body: At Ayen and now Medica Scope I moved that systems thinking into the frontend — composing Angular 21 workspaces in Nx, building reusable configuration-driven libraries, and treating the browser as a first-class runtime for enterprise software.

identityNarrative.heading: Realtime · RTL · Clean Architecture
identityNarrative.body: Today I focus on enterprise LMS work: SignalR realtime streams, bilingual Arabic/English RTL, Standalone Components + Signals, Clean Architecture and SOLID. I also teach MEARN Stack trainees at ITI and mentor 30+ students through their graduation projects.

achievements: Improved page-load speed by 40% across Ayen client projects via lazy loading, code splitting, and image optimization
Reduced REST API response latency by 30% with optimized data-fetching strategies
Decreased production bug rate by 20% through enforced code review and ESLint / Prettier standards
Lifted ITI student graduation project success rate by 35% through mentoring and code refactoring
Boosted Sys Gate ERP module efficiency by 25% and reduced legacy errors by 15%
